audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Numbers / Bemidbar tabernacle transport-duty text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for Kohathite, Gershonite, and Merarite service ages, covering of holy objects, transport boundaries, named oversight, clan counts, and service burdens. Anti-exclusion, worker-safety, and service-without-superiority safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
Lift up head/count sons of Kohath from midst of sons of Levi, by clans and houses of fathers,
from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, all entering host to do work in tent of meeting.
This is service of sons of Kohath in tent of meeting: Holy of Holies things.
When camp sets out, Aaron and his sons shall come and take down veil of screen and cover ark of testimony with it.
They shall put covering of fine leather over it, spread cloth entirely blue above, and put in its poles.
Over table of presence they shall spread blue cloth and put on it dishes, pans, bowls, and jars for drink offering; continual bread shall be upon it.
They shall spread over them scarlet cloth, cover it with covering of fine leather, and put in its poles.
They shall take blue cloth and cover lampstand of light, its lamps, tongs, firepans, and all oil vessels with which they serve it.
They shall put it and all its vessels into covering of fine leather and place it on carrying frame.
Over gold altar they shall spread blue cloth, cover it with covering of fine leather, and put in its poles.
They shall take all vessels of service with which they serve in holy place, put them in blue cloth, cover them with covering of fine leather, and place them on carrying frame.
They shall remove ashes from altar and spread purple cloth over it.
They shall put upon it all its vessels with which they serve: firepans, forks, shovels, basins, all vessels of altar; spread covering of fine leather over it and put in its poles.

When Aaron and his sons finish covering holy place and all vessels of holy place as camp sets out, afterward sons of Kohath shall come to carry, but they shall not touch holy thing, lest they die. These are burden of sons of Kohath in tent of meeting.
Oversight of Eleazar son of Aaron the priest: oil of light, fragrant incense, continual grain offering, anointing oil, oversight of all dwelling-place and all in it, holy place and its vessels.
YHWH spoke to Moses and Aaron, saying,
Do not cut off tribe of clans of Kohathites from midst of Levites.
This do for them, so they may live and not die when they approach Holy of Holies: Aaron and his sons shall come and appoint them, each man to his service and burden.
They shall not go in to see holy things even for a moment, lest they die.
YHWH spoke to Moses, saying,
Lift up head/count sons of Gershon also, by houses of fathers and clans.
From thirty years old and upward to fifty years old you shall count them, all entering to perform service, to do work in tent of meeting.
This is service of clans of Gershonites, to serve and to carry.
They shall carry curtains of dwelling-place, tent of meeting, its covering, covering of fine leather above it, and screen for entrance of tent of meeting,
hangings of courtyard, screen for entrance gate of courtyard around dwelling-place and altar, their cords, and all vessels of their service; all that is made for them, so they shall serve.
By mouth of Aaron and his sons shall be all service of sons of Gershon, all their burdens and service; you shall appoint to them charge of all their burden.
This is service of clans of sons of Gershon in tent of meeting; their charge shall be under hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
Sons of Merari you shall count by clans and houses of fathers.
From thirty years old and upward to fifty years old you shall count them, all entering host to do work of tent of meeting.
This is charge of their burden according to all their service in tent of meeting: frames of dwelling-place, its bars, pillars, and sockets,
pillars of courtyard around, their sockets, pegs, cords, with all vessels and all their service; by names you shall assign vessels of charge of their burden.
This is service of clans of sons of Merari according to all their service in tent of meeting, under hand of Ithamar son of Aaron the priest.
Moses and Aaron and leaders of congregation counted sons of Kohath by clans and houses of fathers,
from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, all entering host for service in tent of meeting.
Those counted by clans were two thousand seven hundred fifty.
These are those counted of clans of Kohathites, all serving in tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron counted by mouth of YHWH by hand of Moses.
Those counted of sons of Gershon by clans and houses of fathers,
from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, all entering host for service in tent of meeting,
those counted by clans and houses of fathers were two thousand six hundred thirty.
These are those counted of clans of sons of Gershon, all serving in tent of meeting, whom Moses and Aaron counted by mouth of YHWH.
Those counted of clans of sons of Merari by clans and houses of fathers,
from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, all entering host for service in tent of meeting,
those counted by clans were three thousand two hundred.
These are those counted of clans of sons of Merari, whom Moses and Aaron counted by mouth of YHWH by hand of Moses.
All those counted of Levites whom Moses and Aaron and leaders of Israel counted by clans and houses of fathers,
from thirty years old and upward to fifty years old, everyone entering to do service of service and service of burden in tent of meeting,
those counted were eight thousand five hundred eighty.
By mouth of YHWH they were counted by hand of Moses, each man according to his service and burden; they were counted as YHWH commanded Moses.

translation_notes: The age range concerns a specific physically demanding transport service, not a measure of spiritual usefulness across the lifespan.
Aaronic covering procedures protect Kohathite workers from direct contact and sight of holy objects; this can be read as worker-safety within ritual danger.
Numbers 4:18-19 explicitly says not to let the Kohathite clans be cut off and assigns clear tasks so they may live.
Each clan receives a named burden and oversight; service is distributed, not concentrated in one elite group.
'Host' language here includes organized labor/service, not only warfare.
safety_note: Do not use Numbers 4 to discard older or younger people, rank forms of labor, or glorify lethal religious gatekeeping. The chapter emphasizes assigned responsibility, safe handling, distributed service, and preserving workers' lives.
